records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2363 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Ibrahim ibn Maymun:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as), "A man plucked the feathers of a pigeon from the pigeons of the Haram."
Imam (as) replied: "He should give charity to a needy person and with the hand that plucked the feathers, for he has caused it pain."
records.arabicText: 2363 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ نَتَفَ حَمَامَةً مِنْ حَمَامِ اَلْحَرَمِ فَقَالَ «يَتَصَدَّقُ بِصَدَقَةٍ عَلَى مِسْكِينٍ وَ يُعْطِي بِالْيَدِ اَلَّتِي نَتَفَ بِهَا فَإِنَّهُ قَدْ أَوْجَعَهُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2363 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Ibrahim ibn Maymun:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as), "A man plucked the feathers of a pigeon from the pigeons of the Haram."
Imam (as) replied: "He should give charity to a needy person and with the hand that plucked the feathers, for he has caused it pain."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2364 - Safwan narrated from Mansur ibn Hazim:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as), "A slaughtered bird was gifted to us in Mecca, and our family ate it."
Imam (as) replied: "The people of Mecca see no harm in it."
I asked: "What do you say about it?"
Imam (as) said: "They are liable for its price."
records.arabicText: 2364 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أُهْدِيَ لَنَا طَيْرٌ مَذْبُوحٌ بِمَكَّةَ فَأَكَلَهُ أَهْلُنَا فَقَالَ «لاَ يَرَى بِهِ أَهْلُ مَكَّةَ بَأْساً» قُلْتُ فَأَيَّ شَيْءٍ تَقُولُ أَنْتَ قَالَ «عَلَيْهِمْ ثَمَنُهُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2364 - Safwan narrated from Mansur ibn Hazim:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as), "A slaughtered bird was gifted to us in Mecca, and our family ate it."
Imam (as) replied: "The people of Mecca see no harm in it."
I asked: "What do you say about it?"
Imam (as) said: "They are liable for its price."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2365 - Safwan narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan:
Abu Abdullah (as) said: "Hunting is not to be slaughtered in the Sacred Sanctuary (Haram), even if it was hunted in the permissible (Hil)."
records.arabicText: 2365 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «لاَ يُذْبَحُ اَلصَّيْدُ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ وَ إِنْ صِيدَ فِي اَلْحِلِّ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2365 - Safwan narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Abu Abdullah (as) said: "Hunting is not to be slaughtered in the Sacred Sanctuary (Haram), even if it was hunted in the permissible (Hil)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2366 - Al-Nadr narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan:
I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say: "For the pigeons of Mecca and the domesticated birds from the pigeons of the Sacred Sanctuary (Haram), whoever slaughters a bird from them must give charity greater than its value. If the person was in the state of Ihram, then a sheep is required for each bird."
records.arabicText: 2366 - وَ رَوَى اَلنَّضْرُ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ : «فِي حَمَامِ مَكَّةَ اَلطَّيْرُ اَلْأَهْلِيُّ مِنْ حَمَامِ اَلْحَرَمِ مَنْ ذَبَحَ مِنْهُ طَيْراً فَعَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَتَصَدَّقَ بِصَدَقَةٍ أَفْضَلَ مِنْ ثَمَنِهِ فَإِنْ كَانَ مُحْرِماً فَشَاةٌ عَنْ كُلِّ طَيْرٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2366 - Al-Nadr narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan:
records.thaqalaynMatn: I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say: "For the pigeons of Mecca and the domesticated birds from the pigeons of the Sacred Sanctuary (Haram), whoever slaughters a bird from them must give charity greater than its value. If the person was in the state of Ihram, then a sheep is required for each bird."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2367 - Muawiyah ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a domesticated bird that entered the Sacred Sanctuary (Haram).
Imam (as) replied: "It should not be taken or touched because Allah (swt), the Almighty, says: 'And whoever enters it shall be safe.'"
records.arabicText: 2367 - وَ سَأَلَ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ طَيْرٍ أَهْلِيٍّ أَقْبَلَ فَدَخَلَ اَلْحَرَمَ فَقَالَ «لاَ يُؤْخَذُ وَ لاَ يُمَسُّ لِأَنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يَقُولُ: «وَ مَنْ دَخَلَهُ كٰانَ آمِناً» ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2367 - Muawiyah ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a domesticated bird that entered the Sacred Sanctuary (Haram).
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It should not be taken or touched because Allah (swt), the Almighty, says: 'And whoever enters it shall be safe.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2368 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked one of the Imams (as)  about a gazelle entering the Sacred Sanctuary (Haram).
Imam (as) replied: "It should not be taken or touched because Allah (swt), the Almighty, says: 'And whoever enters it shall be safe.'"
records.arabicText: 2368 - وَ سَأَلَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ أَحَدَهُمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلظَّبْيِ يَدْخُلُ اَلْحَرَمَ فَقَالَ «لاَ يُؤْخَذُ وَ لاَ يُمَسُّ لِأَنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يَقُولُ: «وَ مَنْ دَخَلَهُ كٰانَ آمِناً» ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2368 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked one of the Imams (as)  about a gazelle entering the Sacred Sanctuary (Haram).
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It should not be taken or touched because Allah (swt), the Almighty, says: 'And whoever enters it shall be safe.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2369 - Ibn Maskan narrated from Yazid ibn Khalifa, who said:
"There was a basket near my house containing two eggs of the Sacred Sanctuary’s pigeons. My servant, unaware of the eggs, overturned the basket, breaking them. I went out and met Abdullah ibn al-Hasan, and I mentioned this to him.
He said: 'Give in charity two handfuls of flour.'
Later, I met Abu Abdullah (as) and informed him.
Imam (as) said: 'He must pay the price of two birds, feeding the pigeons of the Sacred Sanctuary with it.'
I then met Abdullah ibn al-Hasan and informed him of this.
He said: 'He (as) has spoken the truth; follow it, for He (as) has taken this from his forefathers (as).'"
records.arabicText: 2369 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ خَلِيفَةَ قَالَ : كَانَ فِي جَانِبِ بَيْتِي مِكْتَلٌ كَانَ فِيهِ بَيْضَتَانِ مِنْ حَمَامِ اَلْحَرَمِ فَذَهَبَ غُلاَمِي فَكَبَّ اَلْمِكْتَلَ وَ هُوَ لاَ يَعْلَمُ أَنَّ فِيهِ بَيْضَتَيْنِ فَكَسَرَهُمَا فَخَرَجْتُ فَلَقِيتُ عَبْدَ اَللَّهِ بْنَ اَلْحَسَنِ فَذَكَرْتُ ذَلِكَ لَهُ فَقَالَ تَصَدَّقْ بِكَفَّيْنِ مِنْ دَقِيقٍ قَالَ فَلَقِيتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بَعْدُ فَأَخْبَرْتُهُ فَقَالَ لِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «عَلَيْهِ ثَمَنُ طَيْرَيْنِ يُطْعِمُ بِهِ حَمَامَ اَلْحَرَمِ » فَلَقِيتُ عَبْدَ اَللَّهِ بْنَ اَلْحَسَنِ فَأَخْبَرْتُهُ فَقَالَ صَدَقَ خُذْ بِهِ فَإِنَّهُ أَخَذَ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2369 - Ibn Maskan narrated from Yazid ibn Khalifa, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There was a basket near my house containing two eggs of the Sacred Sanctuary’s pigeons. My servant, unaware of the eggs, overturned the basket, breaking them. I went out and met Abdullah ibn al-Hasan, and I mentioned this to him.
He said: 'Give in charity two handfuls of flour.'
Later, I met Abu Abdullah (as) and informed him.
Imam (as) said: 'He must pay the price of two birds, feeding the pigeons of the Sacred Sanctuary with it.'
I then met Abdullah ibn al-Hasan and informed him of this.
He said: 'He (as) has spoken the truth; follow it, for He (as) has taken this from his forefathers (as).'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2370 - Shihab ibn Abd Rabbihi narrated:
"I said to Abu Abdullah (as): 'I partake in Suhoor with chicks brought from outside Mecca, which are slaughtered in the Sacred Sanctuary, and I partake in Suhoor with them.'
Imam (as) replied: 'What an evil Suhoor is your Suhoor! Do you not know that whatever you bring into the Sacred Sanctuary alive becomes forbidden for you to slaughter and to retain?'"
records.arabicText: 2370 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ شِهَابِ بْنِ عَبْدِ رَبِّهِ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنِّي أَتَسَحَّرُ بِفِرَاخٍ أُتِيَ بِهَا مِنْ غَيْرِ مَكَّةَ فَتُذْبَحُ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ فَأَتَسَحَّرُ بِهَا فَقَالَ «بِئْسَ اَلسَّحُورُ سَحُورُكَ أَمَا عَلِمْتَ أَنَّ مَا أَدْخَلْتَ بِهِ اَلْحَرَمَ حَيّاً فَقَدْ حَرُمَ عَلَيْكَ ذَبْحُهُ وَ إِمْسَاكُهُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2370 - Shihab ibn Abd Rabbihi narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I said to Abu Abdullah (as): 'I partake in Suhoor with chicks brought from outside Mecca, which are slaughtered in the Sacred Sanctuary, and I partake in Suhoor with them.'
Imam (as) replied: 'What an evil Suhoor is your Suhoor! Do you not know that whatever you bring into the Sacred Sanctuary alive becomes forbidden for you to slaughter and to retain?'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2371 - Muhammad ibn Humran narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) from his father (as), who said: "I was with Ali ibn al-Husayn (as) in the Sacred Sanctuary, and he saw me troubling the swallows.
Imam (as) said: 'O my son, do not kill them and do not harm them, for they do not harm anything.'"
records.arabicText: 2371 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حُمْرَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «كُنْتُ مَعَ عَلِيِّ بْنِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ بِالْحَرَمِ فَرَآنِي أُوذِي اَلْخَطَاطِيفَ فَقَالَ» «يَا بُنَيَّ لاَ تَقْتُلْهُنَّ وَ لاَ تُؤْذِهِنَّ فَإِنَّهُنَّ لاَ يُؤْذِينَ شَيْئاً».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2371 - Muhammad ibn Humran narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) from his father (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I was with Ali ibn al-Husayn (as) in the Sacred Sanctuary, and he saw me troubling the swallows.
Imam (as) said: 'O my son, do not kill them and do not harm them, for they do not harm anything.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2372 - Abdul Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj narrated:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about two young birds dressed in feathers that I slaughtered while I was in Mecca.
Imam (as) said to me: 'Why did you slaughter them?'
I replied: 'A girl from the people of Mecca brought them to me and asked me to slaughter them, and I thought I was in Kufa and did not recall the sanctity of the Haram.'
Imam (as) said: 'Give charity equivalent to their value.'
I asked: 'How much?'
Imam (as) said: 'A dirham, which is better than them.'"
records.arabicText: 2372 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ اَلْحَجَّاجِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ فَرْخَيْنِ مُسَرْوَلَيْنِ ذَبَحْتُهُمَا وَ أَنَا بِمَكَّةَ فَقَالَ لِي «لِمَ ذَبَحْتَهُمَا» فَقُلْتُ جَاءَتْنِي بِهِمَا جَارِيَةٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ مَكَّةَ فَسَأَلَتْنِي أَنْ أَذْبَحَهُمَا فَظَنَنْتُ أَنِّي بِالْكُوفَةِ وَ لَمْ أَذْكُرِ اَلْحَرَمَ قَالَ «تَصَدَّقْ بِقِيمَتِهِمَا» قُلْتُ كَمْ قَالَ «دِرْهَماً وَ هُوَ خَيْرٌ مِنْهُمَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2372 - Abdul Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about two young birds dressed in feathers that I slaughtered while I was in Mecca.
Imam (as) said to me: 'Why did you slaughter them?'
I replied: 'A girl from the people of Mecca brought them to me and asked me to slaughter them, and I thought I was in Kufa and did not recall the sanctity of the Haram.'
Imam (as) said: 'Give charity equivalent to their value.'
I asked: 'How much?'
Imam (as) said: 'A dirham, which is better than them.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2373 - Zurara narrated:
He asked (Imam (as)) about a man who took a bird from Mecca to Kufa.
The Imam (as) said: "He must return it to Mecca."
records.arabicText: 2373 - وَ سَأَلَهُ زُرَارَةُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَخْرَجَ طَيْراً مِنْ مَكَّةَ إِلَى اَلْكُوفَةِ فَقَالَ «يَرُدُّهُ إِلَى مَكَّةَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2373 - Zurara narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
He asked (Imam (as)) about a man who took a bird from Mecca to Kufa.
The Imam (as) said: "He must return it to Mecca."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2374 - Al-Muthanna narrated from Muhammad ibn Abi al-Hakam:
He said: I told a servant of ours, "Prepare our meal." So he took birds from Mecca, slaughtered them, and cooked them.
I went to Abu Abdullah (as), and Imam (as)  said: "Bury them and offer compensation (fidya) for each bird among them."
records.arabicText: 2374 - وَ رَوَى اَلْمُثَنَّى عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي اَلْحَكَمِ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِغُلاَمٍ لَنَا هَيِّئْ لَنَا غَدَاءَنَا فَأَخَذَ لَنَا مِنْ أَطْيَارِ مَكَّةَ فَذَبَحَهَا وَ طَبَخَهَا فَدَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ «اِدْفِنْهُنَّ وَ اِفْدِ عَنْ كُلِّ طَيْرٍ مِنْهُنَّ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2374 - Al-Muthanna narrated from Muhammad ibn Abi al-Hakam:
He said: I told a servant of ours, "Prepare our meal." So he took birds from Mecca, slaughtered them, and cooked them.
I went to Abu Abdullah (as), and Imam (as)  said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Bury them and offer compensation (fidya) for each bird among them."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2375 - Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Abi Basir, from Abi Abdullah (as):
Regarding a man who killed a bird of the sanctuary while he was in a state of ihram within the Haram,
Imam (as) said: "He must offer a sheep as compensation, and the value of the bird is one dirham, with which he should feed the pigeons of the Haram. If it was a chick, then he must offer a lamb, and the value of the chick is half a dirham, with which he should also feed the pigeons of the Haram."
records.arabicText: 2375 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ قَتَلَ طَيْراً مِنْ طُيُورِ اَلْحَرَمِ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ فَقَالَ «عَلَيْهِ شَاةٌ وَ قِيمَةُ اَلْحَمَامِ دِرْهَمٌ يَعْلِفُ بِهِ حَمَامَ اَلْحَرَمِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ فَرْخاً فَعَلَيْهِ حَمَلٌ وَ قِيمَةُ اَلْفَرْخِ نِصْفُ دِرْهَمٍ يَعْلِفُ بِهِ حَمَامَ اَلْحَرَمِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2375 - Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Abi Basir, from Abi Abdullah (as):
Regarding a man who killed a bird of the sanctuary while he was in a state of ihram within the Haram,
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "He must offer a sheep as compensation, and the value of the bird is one dirham, with which he should feed the pigeons of the Haram. If it was a chick, then he must offer a lamb, and the value of the chick is half a dirham, with which he should also feed the pigeons of the Haram."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2376 - Al-Halabi narrated from Abi Abdullah (as):
"Do not purchase [meat] in the Haram unless it has been slaughtered in the Hill (outside the Haram) and then brought to the Haram after being slaughtered; in such a case, there is no issue with it for someone who is not in a state of ihram."
records.arabicText: 2376 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ تَشْتَرِيَنَّ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ إِلاَّ مَذْبُوحاً قَدْ ذُبِحَ فِي اَلْحِلِّ ثُمَّ جِيءَ بِهِ إِلَى اَلْحَرَمِ مَذْبُوحاً فَلاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ لِلْحَلاَلِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2376 - Al-Halabi narrated from Abi Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not purchase [meat] in the Haram unless it has been slaughtered in the Hill (outside the Haram) and then brought to the Haram after being slaughtered; in such a case, there is no issue with it for someone who is not in a state of ihram."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2377 - Sa'id ibn Abdullah Al-A'raj asked Abu Abdullah (as) about an ostrich egg that he ate while in the Haram.
Imam (as) replied: "Give in charity the equivalent of its price."
records.arabicText: 2377 - وَ سَأَلَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ اَلْأَعْرَجُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ بَيْضَةِ نَعَامَةٍ أَكَلْتُ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ فَقَالَ «تَصَدَّقْ بِثَمَنِهَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2377 - Sa'id ibn Abdullah Al-A'raj asked Abu Abdullah (as) about an ostrich egg that he ate while in the Haram.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Give in charity the equivalent of its price."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2378 - Abdul Rahman ibn Al-Hajjaj narrated that Abu Abdullah (as) said:
"The value of a pigeon is one dirham, the value of a chick is half a dirham, and the value of an egg is a quarter of a dirham."
records.arabicText: 2378 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ اَلْحَجَّاجِ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «فِي قِيمَةِ اَلْحَمَامَةِ دِرْهَمٌ وَ فِي اَلْفَرْخِ نِصْفُ دِرْهَمٍ وَ فِي اَلْبَيْضَةِ رُبُعُ دِرْهَمٍ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2378 - Abdul Rahman ibn Al-Hajjaj narrated that Abu Abdullah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The value of a pigeon is one dirham, the value of a chick is half a dirham, and the value of an egg is a quarter of a dirham."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permitted to Be Slaughtered in the Sanctuary and Taken Outside
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2379 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"It is not permissible to slaughter in the Haram except for camels, cows, sheep, and chickens."
records.arabicText: 2379 - رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ يُذْبَحُ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ إِلاَّ اَلْإِبِلُ وَ اَلْبَقَرُ وَ اَلْغَنَمُ وَ اَلدَّجَاجُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2379 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is not permissible to slaughter in the Haram except for camels, cows, sheep, and chickens."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permitted to Be Slaughtered in the Sanctuary and Taken Outside
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2380 - Mu'awiya ibn Ammar asked him about Ethiopian chickens.
Imam (as) replied: "It is not considered part of the game (ṣayd); game birds are those that fly between the sky and the earth and flap their wings."
records.arabicText: 2380 - وَ سَأَلَهُ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ : عَنْ دَجَاجِ اَلْحَبَشِ فَقَالَ «لَيْسَ مِنَ اَلصَّيْدِ إِنَّمَا اَلطَّيْرُ مَا طَارَ بَيْنَ اَلسَّمَاءِ وَ اَلْأَرْضِ وَ صَفَّ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2380 - Mu'awiya ibn Ammar asked him about Ethiopian chickens.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) replied: "It is not considered part of the game (ṣayd); game birds are those that fly between the sky and the earth and flap their wings."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permitted to Be Slaughtered in the Sanctuary and Taken Outside
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2381 - Jamil ibn Darraj and Muhammad ibn Muslim reported that Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about Sindhi chickens being taken out of the Haram.
Imam (as) said: "Yes, because they cannot fly independently."
In another narration: "They only flutter lightly."
records.arabicText:
2381 - وَ قَالَ جَمِيلُ بْنُ دَرَّاجٍ وَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ : سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلدَّجَاجِ اَلسِّنْدِيِّ يُخْرَجُ بِهِ مِنَ اَلْحَرَمِ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ لِأَنَّهَا لاَ تَسْتَقِلُّ بِالطَّيَرَانِ.
وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ: «أَنَّهَا تَدُفُّ دَفِيفاً ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2381 - Jamil ibn Darraj and Muhammad ibn Muslim reported that Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about Sindhi chickens being taken out of the Haram.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "Yes, because they cannot fly independently."
In another narration: "They only flutter lightly."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permitted to Be Slaughtered in the Sanctuary and Taken Outside
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2382 - Al-Hasan ibn al-Sayqal asked Imam (as) about the chickens and birds of Mecca.
Imam (as) said: "What does not glide (in flight), you may eat it, and what glides, let it go."
records.arabicText: 2382 - وَ سَأَلَهُ اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ اَلصَّيْقَلِ : عَنْ دَجَاجِ مَكَّةَ وَ طَيْرِهَا فَقَالَ «مَا لَمْ يَصُفَّ فَكُلْهُ وَ مَا كَانَ يَصُفُّ فَخَلِّ سَبِيلَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2382 - Al-Hasan ibn al-Sayqal asked Imam (as) about the chickens and birds of Mecca.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "What does not glide (in flight), you may eat it, and what glides, let it go."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permitted to Be Slaughtered in the Sanctuary and Taken Outside
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2383 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a man who brought his cheetah into the sacred sanctuary (Al-Haram). Is he allowed to take it out?
Imam (as) replied: "It is a predator. Whatever predator you bring into the sanctuary as a captive, you are allowed to take it out."
records.arabicText: 2383 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَدْخَلَ فَهْدَهُ إِلَى اَلْحَرَمِ أَ لَهُ أَنْ يُخْرِجَهُ فَقَالَ «هُوَ سَبُعٌ فَكُلُّ مَا أَدْخَلْتَ مِنَ اَلسَّبُعِ اَلْحَرَمَ أَسِيراً فَلَكَ أَنْ تُخْرِجَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2383 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a man who brought his cheetah into the sacred sanctuary (Al-Haram). Is he allowed to take it out?
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is a predator. Whatever predator you bring into the sanctuary as a captive, you are allowed to take it out."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permitted to Be Slaughtered in the Sanctuary and Taken Outside
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2384 - Muawiya ibn Ammar narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that he said: "There is no harm in killing ants and bedbugs in the sacred sanctuary (Al-Haram)."
Imam (as) also said: "There is no harm in killing lice both within the sanctuary and outside of it."
records.arabicText: 2384 - وَ رَوَى عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ بِقَتْلِ اَلنَّمْلِ وَ اَلْبَقِّ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ » وَ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِقَتْلِ اَلْقَمْلَةِ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ وَ غَيْرِهِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2384 - Muawiya ibn Ammar narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There is no harm in killing ants and bedbugs in the sacred sanctuary (Al-Haram)."
Imam (as) also said: "There is no harm in killing lice both within the sanctuary and outside of it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permitted to Be Slaughtered in the Sanctuary and Taken Outside
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2385 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that he said: "Any bird that does not glide in flight is regarded as being like domesticated chickens."
records.arabicText: 2385 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «كُلُّ مَا لَمْ يَصُفَّ مِنَ اَلطَّيْرِ فَهُوَ بِمَنْزِلَةِ اَلدَّجَاجِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2385 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Any bird that does not glide in flight is regarded as being like domesticated chickens."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Travelling for Hajj and Other Acts of Obedience
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2386 - Amr ibn Abi Al-Miqdam narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said: "In the wisdom of the family of Dawood (as) it is stated that it is incumbent upon the intelligent person not to travel except for one of three purposes: gathering provisions for the Hereafter, seeking sustenance for livelihood, or indulging in permissible pleasure."
records.arabicText: 2386 - رَوَى عَمْرُو بْنُ أَبِي اَلْمِقْدَامِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «فِي حِكْمَةِ آلِ دَاوُدَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّ عَلَى اَلْعَاقِلِ أَنْ لاَ يَكُونَ ظَاعِناً إِلاَّ فِي ثَلاَثٍ تَزَوُّدٍ لِمَعَادٍ أَوْ مَرَمَّةٍ لِمَعَاشٍ أَوْ لَذَّةٍ فِي غَيْرِ مُحَرَّمٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2386 - Amr ibn Abi Al-Miqdam narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "In the wisdom of the family of Dawood (as) it is stated that it is incumbent upon the intelligent person not to travel except for one of three purposes: gathering provisions for the Hereafter, seeking sustenance for livelihood, or indulging in permissible pleasure."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Travelling for Hajj and Other Acts of Obedience
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2387 - Al-Sakuni narrated through his chain that the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
"Travel, and you will gain health; strive in the way of Allah (swt), and you will attain victory; and perform Hajj, and you will become self-sufficient."
records.arabicText: 2387 - وَ رَوَى اَلسَّكُونِيُّ بِإِسْنَادِهِ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «سَافِرُوا تَصِحُّوا وَ جَاهِدُوا تَغْنَمُوا وَ حُجُّوا تَسْتَغْنُوا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2387 - Al-Sakuni narrated through his chain that the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Travel, and you will gain health; strive in the way of Allah (swt), and you will attain victory; and perform Hajj, and you will become self-sufficient."
